dialogue_history.content: Nursing Agent, could you please provide details regarding the specific discharge instructions given to the patient, particularly regarding the timeline for dressing removal, showering, and any specific activity restrictions?

The patient was instructed to remove the surgical dressing and begin daily showers following the specific dates provided, while strictly avoiding tub baths or pools until follow-up. Activity restrictions include no lifting over 10 pounds, excessive bending, or twisting for two weeks, along with limiting daily stair use. We also educated the patient on performing daily incision checks for signs of infection and increasing fluid and fiber intake to manage potential constipation from pain medications.

REQUEST TEST: Can you please review the discharge medication list and clarify the instructions regarding NSAID use, as the discharge instructions mention avoiding anti-inflammatories for three months while the medication list includes Ibuprofen? Additionally, could you confirm the dosages for Acetaminophen and Oxycodone?

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule: One (1) Capsule PO BID. 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet: One (1) Tablet PO BID. 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet: ___ Tablets PO Q6H as needed for pain (dosage count not specified in record). 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H as needed for pain. (Note: A conflict exists in the record as Discharge Instructions state: "Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as Motrin, Advil, aspirin, Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months to promote bone fusion.") 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet: ___ Tablets PO Q3H as needed for pain for 2 weeks (dosage count not specified in record). 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule: Two (2) Capsules PO TID. 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY.
Nursing Agent, could you provide more detail on the patient's post-operative mobility and whether she required any physical therapy evaluation or assistance during her stay?

Following surgery, the patient's activity was advanced, and she was documented as ambulating independently and steadily prior to discharge. No specific physical therapy evaluation was recorded, as she was able to mobilize without assistance.

REQUEST TEST: Please clarify the discrepancy regarding Ibuprofen 400 mg (listed as PRN) and the discharge instruction to avoid NSAIDs for 3 months; also, are there specific tablet counts for the PRN Acetaminophen and Oxycodone?

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID. 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID. 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H as needed for pain (Specific tablet count is not specified in the record). 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H as needed for pain (Note: The record contains a discrepancy; while listed here, the Discharge Instructions explicitly state: "Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as Motrin, Advil, aspirin, Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months to promote bone fusion.") 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H as needed for pain for 2 weeks (Specific tablet count is not specified in the record). 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID. 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY.
Nursing Agent, could you provide details on the patient's pain scores during the post-operative period and confirm if there were any issues noted during her Q4 hour neuro checks?

Post-operatively, the patient reported good relief with oral pain medications and was able to advance her activity to independent ambulation. Q4 hour neuro checks showed the patient was awake, alert, and oriented, with only trace weakness in left plantar and dorsiflexion noted upon discharge.

REQUEST TEST: Please clarify if the patient was previously taking Gabapentin or Citalopram prior to admission, or if these were initiated during the hospital stay. Also, check the record for any mention of whether the patient was prescribed 1 or 2 tablets of Acetaminophen and Oxycodone per dose.

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID. 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID. 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H as needed for pain (The record does not specify if 1 or 2 tablets were prescribed; the entry contains a placeholder). 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H as needed for pain (Note: Discharge instructions explicitly state to avoid anti-inflammatories like Ibuprofen for 3 months). 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H as needed for pain for 2 weeks (The record does not specify if 1 or 2 tablets were prescribed; the entry contains a placeholder). 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID (Per the History of Present Illness, the patient was started on "neurontin" [gabapentin] prior to admission, although it was not included in the "Medications on Admission" list). 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (This medication was not listed in the "Medications on Admission" or History of Present Illness).

Nursing Agent, could you check if there were any specific clinical reasons documented for starting the patient on Citalopram during this admission, and could you provide more detail on the specific sensory changes in the left leg that were documented during the final neuro checks?

The nursing records do not provide a specific clinical reason for starting Citalopram during this admission. Regarding sensory changes, final assessments documented slight sensory loss to light touch localized to the left lateral aspect of the foot.

REQUEST TEST: Please check for any clinical notes or a psychiatric history that might explain why Citalopram was started during this admission. Additionally, search for any pharmacy or nursing notes that specify the exact number of tablets for the PRN Acetaminophen and Oxycodone orders.

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID. 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID. 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H as needed for pain (The available record does not specify the number of tablets; a placeholder is present). 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H as needed for pain (Note: This conflicts with Discharge Instructions to avoid NSAIDs for 3 months). 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H as needed for pain for 2 weeks (The available record does not specify the number of tablets; a placeholder is present). 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID. 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (NO INFORMATION AVAILABLE in the clinical notes or psychiatric history to explain the initiation of this medication).
Nursing Agent, were any surgical drains placed during the procedure, and could you provide the specific follow-up appointment timeline or the name of the physician she should see?

There is no mention of surgical drains in the nursing notes, and the incision was documented as clean and dry with Steri-Strips. The specific follow-up timeline and physician name are not specified in the available record.

Nursing Agent, could you please confirm if the patient had a bowel movement prior to discharge, given the prescriptions for docusate and senna? Additionally, was she specifically counseled on the discrepancy regarding avoiding Ibuprofen for bone fusion despite it being listed on her discharge medications?

The nursing records do not specify whether the patient had a bowel movement prior to discharge. While the patient was instructed to avoid anti-inflammatory medications for three months, there is no documentation confirming she was specifically counseled on the discrepancy within her medication list.

REQUEST TEST: Please check the operative note or discharge summary for any mention of a spinal fusion being performed in addition to the discectomy, as the instructions mention avoiding NSAIDs to "promote bone fusion" but the primary procedure listed is a discectomy.

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2 times a day). 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for pain. 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H (every 8 hours) as needed for pain. 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H (every 3 hours) as needed for pain for 2 weeks. 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID (3 times a day). 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). Note regarding the medication instructions: The record identifies the surgical procedure as a "Left L5-S1 discectomy" and "removal of the bulged disc" in the "Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure" and "Brief Hospital Course" sections; there is no mention of a spinal fusion being performed. Despite this, the medication-related discharge instructions state: "Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as Motrin, Advil, aspirin, Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months to promote bone fusion." This instruction conflicts with the inclusion of Ibuprofen 400 mg in the discharge medication list.

Nursing Agent, could you confirm if the patient was specifically educated on the conflicting instructions regarding Ibuprofen, given the "bone fusion" note despite her having a discectomy? Additionally, what was her pain score documented at the time of discharge?

There is no documentation indicating the patient was specifically educated on the discrepancy regarding Ibuprofen and the bone fusion instructions. A specific numerical pain score at discharge is not recorded, though nursing notes state she achieved "good relief" with oral medications.

REQUEST TEST: Please verify if there were any pre-operative medications that were discontinued or held during the hospital stay besides ibuprofen. Nursing Agent, were there any specific discharge teaching materials provided to the patient regarding the management of Gabapentin or the newly started Citalopram?

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID. 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID. 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H as needed for pain. 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H as needed for pain (Note: The Discharge Instructions state "Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as... Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months"). 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H as needed for pain for 2 weeks. 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID. 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY. Regarding pre-operative medications: Per the History of Present Illness, the patient was previously taking a medrol dose pack and naproxen, neither of which were included in the discharge medications. She was also taking neurontin (gabapentin) and oxycodone pre-operatively, which were continued at discharge. During the hospital stay, she was also given decadron for inflammation control, which was not continued at discharge. Regarding discharge teaching: The available record does not contain specific teaching materials or instructions for the management of Gabapentin or Citalopram. General medication instructions advise taking pain medications in the morning for stiffness or before bed for discomfort, and to increase fluid and fiber intake to manage narcotic-induced constipation.

Nursing Agent, since the medication record has placeholders for the number of tablets for Acetaminophen and Oxycodone, could you check the medication administration record (MAR) to see what specific doses were actually administered and well-tolerated by the patient post-operatively? This will help clarify the instructions for her at home.

The available record does not specify the exact number of tablets administered, but nursing notes indicate the patient tolerated oral pain medications with "good relief." This effective pain management allowed her to advance to independent and steady ambulation before discharge.

REQUEST TEST: Please check the laboratory section of the record for any significant post-operative lab values, such as Hemoglobin/Hematocrit or White Blood Cell count, to ensure stability before discharge. Nursing Agent, were there any specific instructions provided to the patient regarding when she could safely resume driving or any other activity restrictions besides the lifting and bending limits already mentioned?

RESULTS: 1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2 times a day). 2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a day). 3. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for pain. 4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H (every 8 hours) as needed for pain. (Note: A conflict exists in the record as Discharge Instructions state: "Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as Motrin, Advil, aspirin, Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months to promote bone fusion.") 5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: ___ Tablets PO Q3H (every 3 hours) as needed for pain for 2 weeks. 6. Gabapentin 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO TID (3 times a day). 7. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily). Regarding the requested laboratory values: No post-operative lab values, such as Hemoglobin/Hematocrit or White Blood Cell counts, are available in the provided hospitalization record; the only pertinent results listed are from a pre-operative MRI. Regarding activity: There is no medication-related information or clinical instruction regarding when the patient could safely resume driving. Non-medication activity restrictions include no pulling, lifting > 10 lbs, or excessive bending/twisting for two weeks, and limiting stairs.

SUMMARY READY: This 29-year-old female with a history of progressive lower back pain and left leg weakness was admitted for surgical management of severe spinal stenosis and a 1 cm L5-S1 disc protrusion. She underwent an uncomplicated left L5-S1 discectomy and demonstrated a smooth post-operative recovery, achieving independent ambulation and good pain control with oral medications. At the time of discharge, she remained neurologically stable with only trace residual weakness in left plantar and dorsiflexion and slight sensory loss in her left foot. She was discharged home in stable condition with a medication regimen including gabapentin, citalopram, docusate, senna, and as-needed analgesics. Her discharge instructions focused on wound care and activity modifications, specifically avoiding lifting more than 10 pounds or excessive bending and twisting for a period of two weeks.
